module_map.responsibility: AST node definitions. Core expression types (Func, BinaryOp, Alias, etc.) with arg_types for type safety. Submodules: core.py (base Expression class), string.py (Levenshtein, Soundex, etc.), query.py (SELECT, INSERT, etc.), dml.py (UPDATE, DELETE), temporal.py (TIMESTAMP, DATE), array.py (ARRAY operations).

SQL string to AST. Consumes tokens, builds expression tree via recursive descent. ~425 functions for parsing distinct SQL constructs. Produces typed Expression instances. Dialect-specific behavior via dialect parsers in parsers/.

AST to SQL string. Traverses expression tree, generates dialect-specific SQL. ~454 generator methods mapping expression types to SQL generation. Handles quoting, formatting, and type-specific rendering.

Tokenization layer. Converts SQL string to token stream. Token types: IDENTIFIER, KEYWORD, NUMBER, STRING, OPERATOR, etc. tokenizer_core.py contains core lexing logic; tokens.py wraps it with dialect-specific rules.

Dialect-specific parsing and generation. Each dialect file (bigquery.py, postgres.py, snowflake.py, etc.) subclasses Parser and Generator, overriding parsing rules and output formatting. Handles dialect-specific functions (e.g., EDITDISTANCE -> LEVENSHTEIN mapping).

AST transformation and optimization. Modules: annotate_types.py (type inference), qualify_columns.py (scope resolution), simplify.py (constant folding), push_predicates.py (WHERE optimization). Operates on expression trees post-parse.

Query execution engine for standalone execution (no database). python.py interprets expressions into Python operations. context.py manages execution state. Limited to expressions, not full SQL semantics.

types_produced: Expression subclasses (Select, Insert, Func, Binary, Literal, etc.)
Annotated Expression tree (types inferred, columns qualified)

pipeline_trace.module: generator.py (+ dialects/[dialect].py for dialect output rules)

cross_module_hubs.reason: Base class Expression. Imported by parser.py, generator.py, optimizer/* modules, and all expression submodules. Central hub; all AST work depends on it.
Imports from expressions, tokens, errors, helper, time, trie. All dialect parsers inherit from it. Called by __init__.py public API. High coupling to tokenizer and expression modules.
Base Dialect class. All dialect implementations subclass it. Imported by parser.py, generator.py, tokens.py for dialect resolution. Central registry of all supported dialects.
Depends on expressions core, helper, schema. Called by public transpile API. All dialect generators inherit from it. Generates SQL from AST.
Scope resolution for optimizer. Imported by qualify_columns.py, resolver.py, schema analysis. Complex interdependencies within optimizer module.

files_to_modify: sqlglot/expressions/string.py - Add LEVENSHTEIN(str1, str2, [max_distance], [ins_cost], [del_cost]) function class if not already present
sqlglot/parsers/*.py - Dialect-specific parsers that lack LEVENSHTEIN/EDITDISTANCE/LEVENSHTEINDISTANCE mapping (e.g., mysql.py, mariadb.py, sqlite.py)
sqlglot/dialects/*.py - Corresponding generator overrides for dialect-specific LEVENSHTEIN output (e.g., generate EDITDISTANCE for Snowflake)
tests/dialects/test_*.py - Add test cases for LEVENSHTEIN transpilation across dialects
new_types: Likely none - Levenshtein already exists in expressions/string.py with arg_types for (this, expression, ins_cost, del_cost, max_distance). May need to add max_distance support to dialects that lack it (e.g., Snowflake's EDITDISTANCE third arg).
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow Soundex/Levenshtein precedent: (1) Expression class in expressions/string.py defines arg_types with all variant parameters. (2) Each dialect parser adds FUNCTION_NAME -> Expression.from_arg_list entry in FUNCTIONS dict. (3) Each dialect generator adds case in sql_gen_method to output dialect-specific name and arguments. (4) Tests validate round-trip transpilation.
change_proposal.integration_point: LEVENSHTEIN is already a hub function in sqlglot. New dialects or parameter variants hook into (1) parsers/[dialect].py FUNCTIONS mapping, (2) dialects/[dialect].py Generator.sql_* method override, (3) test fixtures for round-trip validation. No core pipeline changes needed.
risks: Parameter variance across dialects: PostgreSQL uses LEVENSHTEIN(str1, str2), Snowflake EDITDISTANCE(str1, str2, [max_dist]), ClickHouse EDITDISTANCE(str1, str2). Cost parameters (ins_cost, del_cost) are dialect-specific. Ensure arg_types in Expression match all variants or add fallback.
NULL handling: Different dialects handle NULL arguments differently (some return NULL, some return distance). Optimizer/executor may need dialect-aware NULL semantics.
Performance: Levenshtein is O(n*m) in input length. Large string columns may not benefit from code-analyze tracing. No existing executor optimization visible; verify executor/python.py supports it.

Missing dialect coverage: SQLite, MariaDB, MySQL may lack native LEVENSHTEIN; transpilation to unsupported dialect may fail silently or produce incorrect output. Add error handling or downgrade to SOUNDEX if unavailable.

tool_usage.params: path=/tmp/sqlglot, summary=true, max_depth=2 - mapped repo structure, identified 64 files across dialects, optimizer, executor
path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot/tokens.py, summary=true - extracted Tokenizer class, token flow, imports from tokenizer_core
path=/tmp/sqlglot/sqlglot, symbol=Expression, follow_depth=1 - traced Expression base class definition in expressions/core.py
grep -r 'LEVENSHTEIN|EDITDISTANCE|SOUNDEX' --include='*.py' - identified 27 references across 10 dialect files, tests, and generators. Found Levenshtein class in expressions/string.py with arg_types.

Listed sqlglot/ structure: found expressions/ submodule, optimizer/, executor/, dialects/, parser.py, generator.py, tokens.py. Identified key files: expressions/string.py (Levenshtein definition), parsers/* (dialect-specific parsing).
